tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and glycosuria, suggesting possible renal impairment and poor glycemic control (HbA1c 6.1%). Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and positive for ketones and bilirubin. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Findings are consistent with early diabetic nephropathy and possible urinary tract pathology; further nephrological and diabetic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
